message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1). Venv: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
/tmp/pass1_reconfirm.json is a JSON array of 10 pass-1 findings that were strengthened after a first confirmation pass found them 'appropriate but incomplete'. Each item has: issue, title, file, problem, fix_update (what was just added/changed).
Constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign, no metered API, engine imports no adapter, lazy optional backends.

You are independent CONFIRMER #1 of 2 for INDEX 6 (0-based) in /tmp/pass1_reconfirm.json.
Read that item (issue, problem, fix_update). Then verify the CURRENT state on branch harden/pass-1: read the changed source AND the named regression test(s), and run a PoC / the specific tests against the venv where useful. Decide: appropriate = the fix soundly addresses the root cause (no hack/over-claim); complete = it fully resolves the finding AND has a regression test that would actually catch a regression (a test that passes equally with/without the fix does NOT count). If the item documents a deliberately-deferred scope (e.g. #119's Protocol-boundary note), judge completeness against that honestly-scoped fix, not an unbounded ideal. Set either false with specifics if not met. Echo the issue number.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
{
  "issue": "119",
  "title": "corpus_fingerprint is recomputed redundantly per study (ingest + every build_baseline_artifact call), each a full canonical copy + sort + JSON of the whole corpus",
  "file": "src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py",
  "sev": "LOW",
  "problem": "corpus_fingerprint builds a full list-comprehension copy of every unit and relation, sorts all units by id, canonicalizes every attribute mapping via _plain, and json.dumps the whole thing \u2014 O(total corpus bytes) time and memory. run_study computes it once at ingest (pipeline.py:280, `cfp`). Then build_baseline_artifact (determinism.py:104) computes it again from scratch for the determinism record's `input_hashes['corpus']` and `corpus_fingerprint`; a study's BaselineBuilder that calls build_bas",
  "fix_update": "build_baseline_artifact accepts corpus_hash (no recompute); NEW test test_build_baseline_reuses_precomputed_corpus_hash. NOTE: full e2e dedup across the BaselineBuilder Protocol boundary is deliberately deferred (would require a breaking build() signature change for a LOW perf item) \u2014 documented scope."
}

1	"""Determinism & reproducibility harness for the baseline (ADR-0001, METHODOLOGY.md §1, §7).
2	
3	A baseline is only a yardstick if the *same inputs reproduce the same baseline*. This module
4	makes that contract concrete and engine-level: it content-hashes inputs, derives seeds
5	deterministically from those hashes, records component versions, and stamps all of it into a
6	:class:`~assay_engine.baseline.toolkit.BaselineArtifact`'s ``determinism`` record — so a
7	hostile reviewer can confirm a baseline was produced exactly as claimed.
8	
9	It is dependency-free and pure. The heavy, *choice-bearing* builders (which embedding model,
10	which clustering algorithm, which graph construction) encode dataset/study decisions and are
11	supplied by a study's adapter as :class:`~assay_engine.baseline.toolkit.BaselineBuilder`
12	implementations — not prescribed by the engine (ADR-0002). This harness records *how* such a
13	builder ran so the result is reproducible regardless of which builder it was.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import hashlib
19	import json
20	import sys
21	from dataclasses import dataclass
22	from typing import Any, Mapping
23	
24	from assay_engine import __version__ as _ENGINE_VERSION
25	
26	# The canonical hashing primitives moved to ``assay_engine._canonical`` (see note below); these
27	# imports re-export them at their historical home so existing call sites keep working.
28	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_plain as _plain  # noqa: F401
29	from assay_engine._canonical import hash_bytes as hash_bytes  # noqa: F401  (re-export)
30	from assay_engine._canonical import hash_text
31	from assay_engine._canonical import hash_value as hash_value  # re-export (baseline public API)
32	from assay_engine._canonical import keytag as _keytag  # noqa: F401
33	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
34	from assay_engine.baseline.toolkit import BaselineArtifact
35	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
36	
37	# The canonical hashing primitives (``_plain``/``_keytag``/``hash_bytes``/``hash_text``/
38	# ``hash_value``) live in ``assay_engine._canonical`` so they sit below both ``baseline`` and
39	# ``methodology`` in the import graph (audit #B1/#B2/#B5 originally landed here; hoisted so
40	# pre-registration can content-bind a hypothesis with the *same* type-faithful digest). They are
41	# re-exported above for back-compat.
42	
43	
44	def corpus_fingerprint(corpus: Corpus) -> str:
45	    """Deterministic content hash of a corpus (order-independent across units/relations)."""
46	    units = [
47	        {"id": u.unit_id, "text": u.text, "attrs": _plain(u.attributes)}
48	        for u in sorted(corpus.units, key=lambda u: u.unit_id)
49	    ]
50	    relations = [
51	        {"s": r.source_id, "t": r.target_id, "k": r.kind, "attrs": _plain(r.attributes)}
52	        for r in sorted(corpus.relations, key=lambda r: (r.source_id, r.target_id, r.kind))
53	    ]
54	    payload = {"units": units, "relations": relations, "metadata": _plain(corpus.metadata)}
55	    return hash_text(json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, allow_nan=False))
56	
57	
58	def stable_seed(*parts: str, bits: int = 32) -> int:
59	    """A deterministic non-negative integer seed derived from ``parts``.
60	
61	    Derived from a hash so the same inputs always yield the same seed — reproducible without a
62	    hard-coded magic number, and distinct inputs get distinct seeds.
63	    """
64	    digest = hashlib.sha256("\x1f".join(parts).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
65	    return int(digest, 16) % (1 << bits)
66	
67	
68	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
69	class DeterminismRecord:
70	    """The reproducibility provenance stamped onto a baseline (ADR-0001)."""
71	
72	    seed: int
73	    input_hashes: Mapping[str, str]
74	    component_versions: Mapping[str, str]
75	
76	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
77	        object.__setattr__(self, "input_hashes", freeze_mapping(self.input_hashes))
78	        object.__setattr__(self, "component_versions", freeze_mapping(self.component_versions))
79	
80	    def as_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
81	        return {
82	            "seed": self.seed,
83	            "input_hashes": dict(self.input_hashes),
84	            "component_versions": dict(self.component_versions),
85	        }
86	
87	
88	def build_baseline_artifact(
89	    corpus: Corpus,
90	    contents: Mapping[str, Any],
91	    *,
92	    component_versions: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
93	    extra_inputs: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
94	    seed: int | None = None,
95	    corpus_hash: str | None = None,
96	) -> BaselineArtifact:
97	    """Assemble a :class:`BaselineArtifact` with a complete determinism record.
98	
99	    ``contents`` is the builder's output (embeddings, similarity graph, clusters, …).
100	    ``component_versions`` records the builder + any model/library versions (the engine and
101	    Python versions are added automatically). ``extra_inputs`` are additional inputs to hash
102	    (e.g. config). ``seed``, if omitted, is derived deterministically from the corpus + inputs
103	    so it is reproducible. ``corpus_hash`` lets a caller pass an already-computed
104	    :func:`corpus_fingerprint` to avoid recomputing it (#119).
105	    """
106	    # Reuse a precomputed fingerprint if the caller already has one (e.g. the runner computed it
107	    # at ingest), avoiding a redundant full canonical-hash of the whole corpus (#119).
108	    corpus_hash = corpus_hash if corpus_hash is not None else corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
109	    if extra_inputs and "corpus" in extra_inputs:
110	        raise ValueError("'corpus' is a reserved input-hash key — rename the extra input (#B7)")
111	    input_hashes = {"corpus": corpus_hash}
112	    for name, value in (extra_inputs or {}).items():
113	        input_hashes[name] = hash_value(value)
114	
115	    versions = {"engine": _ENGINE_VERSION, "python": sys.version.split()[0]}
116	    versions.update(component_versions or {})
117	
118	    if seed is None:
119	        # Derive from all input hashes (the corpus hash is already among them under "corpus").
120	        seed = stable_seed(*(input_hashes[k] for k in sorted(input_hashes)))
121	
122	    record = DeterminismRecord(seed=seed, input_hashes=input_hashes, component_versions=versions)
123	    return BaselineArtifact(
124	        corpus_fingerprint=corpus_hash, contents=contents, determinism=record.as_dict()
125	    )
126

content.text: The fix is implemented. Now let me find and read the regression test.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && grep -rn "test_build_baseline_reuses_precomputed_corpus_hash" tests/ 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; grep -rln "build_baseline_artifact" tests/ 2>/dev/null

content.content:
255	
256	def test_cosine_matrix_array_matches_list_and_is_ndarray():
257	    # #106: the ndarray variant avoids nested-list boxing but agrees with the list form
258	    np = pytest.importorskip("numpy")
259	    from assay_engine.baseline.primitives import (
260	        cosine_similarity_matrix,
261	        cosine_similarity_matrix_array,
262	    )
263	
264	    rows = [[1.0, 0.0], [0.0, 1.0], [1.0, 1.0]]
265	    arr = cosine_similarity_matrix_array(rows)
266	    assert isinstance(arr, np.ndarray) and arr.shape == (3, 3)
267	    assert arr.tolist() == cosine_similarity_matrix(rows)
268	    assert cosine_similarity_matrix_array([]).shape == (0, 0)
269	
270	
271	def test_build_baseline_reuses_precomputed_corpus_hash(monkeypatch):
272	    # #119: a caller that already has the fingerprint can pass it to skip the redundant recompute
273	    import assay_engine.baseline.determinism as d
274	
275	    corpus = Corpus(units=(Unit("u0", "t0"), Unit("u1", "t1")))
276	    calls = {"n": 0}
277	    orig = d.corpus_fingerprint
278	
279	    def counting(c):
280	        calls["n"] += 1
281	        return orig(c)
282	
283	    monkeypatch.setattr(d, "corpus_fingerprint", counting)
284	    art = d.build_baseline_artifact(corpus, {"k": 1}, corpus_hash="deadbeef" * 8)
285	    assert art.corpus_fingerprint == "deadbeef" * 8 and calls["n"] == 0  # no recompute
286	    d.build_baseline_artifact(corpus, {"k": 1})  # without it → recomputes
287	    assert calls["n"] == 1
288	
289	
290	def test_freeze_keeps_ndarray_opaque_o1():
291	    # #107: a numpy array in baseline contents is frozen to a small (kind, shape, bytes)
292	    # descriptor — NOT a recursively tuple-ized cell-by-cell copy.
293	    np = pytest.importorskip("numpy")
294	    from assay_engine._frozen import freeze
295	
296	    frozen = freeze(np.zeros((100, 100)))
297	    # descriptor form: a 3-tuple (kind, shape, frozen-bytes), not a 100-deep nested structure
298	    assert isinstance(frozen, tuple) and len(frozen) == 3
299	    assert frozen[1] == (100, 100)
300

content.text: The test is well-designed: it monkeypatches `corpus_fingerprint` with a counting wrapper, then asserts that passing `corpus_hash` results in zero calls (and uses the passed value), while omitting it triggers exactly one recompute. This would fail without the fix (without `corpus_hash` param, calls would be 1 in the first case and the artifact wouldn't carry "deadbeef"). Let me verify the test imports and run it.

content.text: Now let me verify the test actually catches a regression by simulating the unfixed state (removing the `corpus_hash` parameter handling).

p="src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py"

s=s.replace(
'    corpus_hash = corpus_hash if corpus_hash is not None else corpus_fingerprint(corpus)',
'    corpus_hash = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)')
open(p,"w").write(s)
EOF
.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_baseline.py::test_build_baseline_reuses_precomputed_corpus_hash -q 2>&1 | tail -8
cp /tmp/det_backup.py src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py
echo "RESTORED"

The test correctly fails without the fix (proving it's a real regression test, not a tautology) and passes with it restored.

Now let me verify the deferred-scope claim is honest. The note says full e2e dedup across the BaselineBuilder Protocol boundary is deferred. Let me check whether `run_study` actually passes the precomputed hash through, and confirm the Protocol boundary claim.

content.text:
This confirms the deferred-scope claim precisely. At pipeline.py:355, `plan.baseline_builder.build(corpus, claim_guard=guard)` is the `BaselineBuilder` Protocol boundary. The builder internally calls `build_baseline_artifact` and computes its own fingerprint, then pipeline.py:356 cross-checks it against `cfp`. To pass `cfp` through to the builder so it could forward it as `corpus_hash` would indeed require a breaking change to the `build()` signature. So full e2e dedup is genuinely deferred, exactly as documented.

The fix soundly addresses the root cause at the function level (the API now accepts a precomputed hash), and the deferral is honestly scoped (a LOW perf item not worth a breaking Protocol change). The test catches the regression. Let me confirm the full test file passes for safety.

content.text: All 29 tests pass. My verification is complete.

input.reasoning:
Issue #119 (LOW perf): corpus_fingerprint recomputed redundantly. CONFIRMER #1 verdict: appropriate=true, complete=true (judged against the honestly-deferred scope).

APPROPRIATE: The fix soundly addresses the root cause at the function API level. build_baseline_artifact (determinism.py:88-125) now accepts a keyword-only corpus_hash param; line 108 reuses it when provided (`corpus_hash if corpus_hash is not None else corpus_fingerprint(corpus)`), eliminating the redundant full O(corpus-bytes) canonical sort+JSON+hash. The passed hash flows correctly into both input_hashes['corpus'] (line 111) and the artifact's corpus_fingerprint field (line 124). No hack, no over-claim. The seed derivation still works because the corpus hash is among input_hashes.

DEFERRAL IS HONESTLY SCOPED: I verified the documented deferral is real, not an excuse. pipeline.py:355 calls plan.baseline_builder.build(corpus, claim_guard=guard) — the BaselineBuilder Protocol boundary. The builder internally produces its own fingerprint; pipeline.py:356 cross-checks baseline.corpus_fingerprint against the ingest-time cfp (line 299). Threading cfp into the builder so it could forward corpus_hash would indeed require a breaking change to build()'s signature — not justified for a LOW perf item. The fix_update note states exactly this. Judged against that honestly-scoped fix (improve the helper API, defer cross-Protocol e2e dedup), the finding is fully resolved.

COMPLETE / REAL REGRESSION TEST: test_build_baseline_reuses_precomputed_corpus_hash (tests/test_baseline.py:271-287) monkeypatches corpus_fingerprint with a call-counting wrapper, asserts passing corpus_hash='deadbeef'*8 yields calls==0 AND the artifact carries that exact hash, then asserts omitting it triggers exactly one recompute. I proved it is a genuine regression test, not a tautology: reverting line 108 to always recompute makes the test FAIL (calls==1 and artifact fingerprint mismatches deadbeef); restoring the fix makes it pass.
input.evidence: determinism.py:88-125 (corpus_hash param added; line 108 reuse logic; lines 111,124 propagate). tests/test_baseline.py:271-287 (regression test). pipeline.py:299,355-360 (confirms BaselineBuilder.build() Protocol boundary => deferral genuine). Test run: test passes (1 passed). Mutation check: reverting line 108 to `corpus_hash = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)` => FAILED (calls['n']==1, fingerprint=1e82ffe7... != deadbeef*8); restored => 29 passed.

content.text:
Confirmed INDEX 6 / issue #119. Verdict: appropriate=true, complete=true.

The fix (build_baseline_artifact accepting a precomputed corpus_hash to skip the redundant full-corpus rehash) soundly addresses the root cause at the function API level, and the deferral of full e2e dedup across the BaselineBuilder Protocol boundary is genuinely necessary (it would require a breaking build() signature change for a LOW perf item) and honestly documented. The regression test is real — it fails when I revert the fix and passes when restored.
